answers: "{\"current_systems\":\"Daily operations run through EFI Pace for job tickets, estimating, and basic scheduling, but we still rely heavily on a physical production board for real-time adjustments. Prepress uses Adobe Creative Cloud and internal proofing workflows. File intake comes through FTP, email, and WeTransfer. Slack and email are used for coordination. Excel is used frequently for capacity planning and margin tracking. QuickBooks handles accounting and payroll.\",\"tech_stack\":\"ERP / MIS: EFI Pace\\n\\nCRM: HubSpot (sales-managed)\\n\\nAccounting: QuickBooks Enterprise\\n\\nDesign / Prepress: Adobe Creative Cloud, Enfocus\\n\\nFile Management: FTP server, WeTransfer, OneDrive\\n\\nCommunication: Microsoft 365, Slack\\n\\nReporting: Excel (manual exports from EFI Pace)\\n\\nOn-prem servers for production data storage\",\"automation_level\":\"basic\",\"pain_points\":\"Manual scheduling adjustments consume significant time. Sales-to-production handoffs often require clarification, causing delays. Estimating bottlenecks during peak seasons create ripple effects. Rework due to incomplete specs or artwork errors impacts press time and margins. Capacity planning and margin tracking require manual spreadsheet consolidation.\",\"data_quality\":\"Data is fragmented across systems. EFI Pace contains job data, HubSpot has pipeline data, and QuickBooks holds financials. Real-time visibility into margin by job type, capacity utilization, and on-time performance is limited. Reporting requires manual exports and reconciliation.\",\"integration_challenges\":\"HubSpot does not sync automatically with EFI Pace, leading to duplicate entry. Scheduling changes made on the production board are not reflected in digital systems in real time. Financial data is not directly tied to operational job data for margin analysis. Overall, there is no unified dashboard showing end-to-end performance.\"}"

"{\"sales_process\":\"Leads come in through the website, referrals, or inbound calls. An inside rep logs the opportunity in HubSpot and assigns it to an outside rep. The rep gathers specs, submits a quote request to estimating, reviews pricing, then sends the proposal. Follow-up cadence depends on the rep. For larger accounts, we may go through multiple rounds of revisions before close. Once approved, the job is handed off to production via EFI Pace entry.\",\"lead_generation\":\"Referrals from existing customers (strongest channel)\\n\\nWebsite inquiries (moderate volume, mixed quality)\\n\\nOutside rep prospecting\\n\\nTrade shows and local business associations\\n\\nLimited paid digital campaigns (inconsistent ROI)\",\"crm_tools\":\"HubSpot CRM (inconsistent usage across reps)\\n\\nEmail (Microsoft 365)\\n\\nExcel for custom pricing models\\n\\nEFI Pace for formal estimating\\n\\nSlack for quick internal coordination\\n\\nWe lack structured automation for follow-up sequences.\",\"conversion_challenges\":\"Slow quote turnaround during peak season. Incomplete job specs from prospects. Inconsistent follow-up discipline across reps. Limited visibility into why deals are lost (price vs. timing vs. scope). Handoff friction between sales and production can also delay momentum.\",\"customer_insights\":\"We track basic pipeline stages and revenue by rep, but deeper insights into margin by client type, lifetime value, and repeat frequency require manual reporting. I wish we had clearer data on win/loss reasons, average sales cycle length by segment, and quote-to-close ratios by job type.\",\"automation_opportunities\":\"Automated lead intake and prequalification. Structured follow-up sequences for quotes and dormant leads. AI-assisted quote preparation based on historical job data. Real-time dashboarding for pipeline health and rep activity consistency.\"}"

"{\"current_systems\":\"We use EFI Pace for estimating and job management, but scheduling is still largely managed on a physical production board with manual adjustments. HubSpot is used by sales for pipeline tracking, though adoption is inconsistent. Prepress relies on Adobe Creative Cloud and Enfocus PitStop. File transfers happen via FTP and WeTransfer. Internally we use Slack and email for communication. QuickBooks Enterprise handles accounting, and most production reporting is exported into Excel for analysis.\",\"tech_stack\":\"CRM: HubSpot ad some people use Salesforce - and other tools\\n\\nERP / MIS: EFI Pace\\n\\nAccounting: QuickBooks Enterprise\\n\\nDesign / Prepress: Adobe Creative Cloud, Enfocus\\n\\nFile Transfer: FTP server, WeTransfer\\n\\nCommunication: Microsoft 365, Slack\\n\\nReporting: Excel (manual exports from EFI Pace)\\n\\nOn-prem production servers + limited cloud storage (OneDrive)\",\"automation_level\":\"basic\",\"pain_points\":\"Production scheduling and capacity planning take the most time. We manually reconcile job specs between sales and production, and incomplete information often forces back-and-forth clarification. Estimating during peak season becomes a bottleneck. Rework due to artwork or spec errors consumes unnecessary press time. We also spend too much time manually tracking job status for internal updates and client follow-ups.\",\"data_quality\":\"Data is fragmented. EFI Pace contains job data, HubSpot has lead and pipeline data, and financials sit in QuickBooks. Reporting requires manual exports and spreadsheet consolidation. Real-time visibility into capacity, margin by job type, and on-time delivery performance is limited.\",\"integration_challenges\":\"HubSpot does not sync cleanly with EFI Pace, so sales data must be manually re-entered into estimating. Scheduling changes made on the physical board are not reflected automatically in systems. Production data and financial data are not unified, which makes margin analysis and forecasting slower and less reliable.\"}"

"{\"top_priorities\":\"Increase revenue from $15.4M to $18M without adding major headcount\\nImprove gross margin by 3–5% through tighter scheduling and reduced rework\\nShorten quote turnaround to under 24 hours consistently\",\"biggest_frustration\":\"We’ve grown revenue, but our systems haven’t matured at the same pace. I still get pulled into production decisions, scheduling escalations, and large-client issues that should be handled at the manager level. Quoting and job handoffs are inconsistent, and that creates unnecessary fire drills.\",\"ideal_state\":\"Jobs would move cleanly from sales to production with zero ambiguity. Quotes would go out within 24 hours. I’d spend my time on growth, partnerships, and capital planning instead of troubleshooting daily operations.\",\"workflow_stuck\":\"Sales-to-production handoff is the biggest friction point. Missing specs, unclear timelines, or incomplete art files delay scheduling. Approvals for large jobs also stall while waiting on me or Dana.\",\"team_bottlenecks\":\"COO (Dana) – juggling scheduling, staffing, and vendor issues\\nProduction scheduling – too dependent on manual board\\nEstimating team during peak season\",\"owner_bottlenecks\":\"Final pricing approvals on larger accounts, resolving scheduling conflicts, stepping in on escalated client complaints, and reviewing capital purchase decisions that should be pre-vetted.\",\"systems_struggles\":\"HubSpot CRM – reps use it differently or not at all.\\nEFI Pace – powerful but underutilized.\\nSlack – informal, not structured.\",\"communication_breakdown\":\"Internally between sales and production. With clients, delays happen when artwork corrections or proof approvals aren’t clearly tracked.\",\"manual_firefighting\":\"Scheduling and capacity planning. Also manually transferring data from CRM into estimating and production systems.\",\"growth_barriers\":\"Inconsistent quoting speed and lack of clean pipeline visibility.\",\"volume_breaking_point\":\"Estimating and scheduling would collapse under volume.\",\"ai_opportunities\":\"Automating lead intake and qualification, standardizing quoting inputs, and improving production scheduling visibility.\",\"if_nothing_else_changes_but_X_this_was_worth_it\":\"If I can step out of daily operational firefighting and focus on growth, this was worth it.\",\"role_label\":\"Owner\",\"department_key\":\"owner\",\"display_name\":\"Mark Ellison\",\"preferred_reference\":\"Mark Ellison\",\"top_3_issues\":[\"Clean, fast, and accurate quoting process\",\"Reliable sales-to-production handoffs\",\"Reduce my involvement in daily operational decisions\"],\"primary_kpis\":[\"Quote turnaround time\",\"Gross margin\",\"On-time job completion\"],\"kpi_baselines\":{\"Quote turnaround time\":\"24–72 hours\",\"Gross margin\":\"18% blended\",\"On-time job completion\":\"~84%\"},\"change_readiness\":\"medium\",\"weekly_capacity_for_implementation_hours\":8,\"biggest_risk_if_we_push_too_fast\":\"Disrupting production flow during peak season and overwhelming managers who are already stretched thin.\"}"
